title: Construct Binary Tree from Traversals
description:
Given two arrays representing the **preorder** and **inorder** traversal of a binary tree, reconstruct the tree and return it.

The tree is represented as nested arrays: `[val, left, right]`, where `left` and `right` are either nested arrays or `null`.

Example: preorder `[3,9,20,15,7]`, inorder `[9,3,15,20,7]` → `[3,[9,null,null],[20,[15,null,null],[7,null,null]]]`
hints: The first element of preorder is always the root.
Find the root's position in inorder — everything left is left subtree, everything right is right subtree.
Recurse on the left and right portions of both arrays.
